message:
feat(core): land large engine update across runtime, recorder/search, and test infra

This commit batches the full  tree update as one logical engine-layer change set. It captures concurrent core work that was already present in the working tree and keeps all related library/test/bench artifacts together.

Highlights included in this grouping:

- Runtime/control-plane internals: expanded  behavior and metrics surfaces, additional scheduler/backpressure-related plumbing, and new lock-free SPSC channel implementation () with dedicated benchmarks and verification tests.

- Recorder/query/search stack evolution: updates across recorder modules (), Tantivy ingestion/query/reindex/policy/quality modules, and hybrid search/reranker paths so retrieval, retention, replay, and export flows evolve consistently.

- Workflow/account/caut internals in core: account selection/quota logic, caut integration paths, and workflow-side structures were updated in lockstep with storage/policy/error surfaces where those types are consumed.

- IPC/web/native integration surfaces: core-side web/IPC/native-event/watcher client modules were updated together with vendored mux client/pool code paths to keep transport/state handling coherent.

- Test and benchmark expansion: broad proptest/integration/loom coverage updates plus new benches for PDU pipelining/SPSC/subscription streaming, along with cross-module test fixture updates in  and .

Why grouped this way:

- All staged files are under one crate boundary () and represent shared engine semantics. Splitting these paths further would fragment type-coupled changes and make bisect/debug of core behavior harder.

- Generated ephemeral regression artifacts were intentionally excluded from this commit ().

+[[bench]]

+harness = false

+
 [[bench]]

 harness = false
